ddls:
CREATE TABLE "books" (
  "book_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each book */,
  "published_year" INTEGER /* Year the book was published */,
  "book_name" TEXT /* Title of the book */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the author of the book */,
  "genre" TEXT /* Genre of the book */,
  "isbn" TEXT /* International Standard Book Number */,
  "publisher" TEXT /* Name of the publisher */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the book */,
  "pages" INTEGER /* Number of pages in the book */,
  "synopsis" TEXT /* Brief summary of the book */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("book_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cases" (
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each case */,
  "case_name" TEXT /* Name of the case as presented in the book */,
  "time_of_case" TEXT /* Specific time when the case occurred */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year when the case took place */,
  "place" TEXT /* Location where the case was centered */,
  "book_id" INTEGER /* ID of the book the case is associated with */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the case */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Description of how the case was resolved */,
  "main_characters" TEXT /* Main characters involved in the case */,
  "evidence" TEXT /* Key pieces of evidence in the case */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cases_book_id FOREIGN KEY ("book_id") REFERENCES books ("book_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "book_cases" (
  "book_id" INTEGER /* ID of the book related to the case */,
  "case_id" INTEGER /* ID of the case linked to the book */,
  "chapter" TEXT /* Specific chapter where the case is discussed */,
  "page_numbers" TEXT /* Page numbers where the case is detailed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("book_id", "case_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., author, editor, researcher) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Brief biography or profile of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "account_status" TEXT /* Status of the user's account (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analyses" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user conducting the analysis */,
  "book_id" INTEGER /* ID of the book being analyzed */,
  "case_id" INTEGER /* ID of the case being analyzed */,
  "analysis_text" TEXT /* Text of the analysis or commentary provided by the user */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the user (1-5) */,
  "tags" TEXT /* Tags or keywords for the analysis */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date when the analysis was made */,
  "revisions" TEXT /* Revisions or updates to the analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analyses_book_id FOREIGN KEY ("book_id") REFERENCES books ("book_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analyses_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("case_id") REFERENCES cases ("case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analyses_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the notification message */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., new book, new analysis tool) */,
  "read_status" INTEGER /* Read status of the notification (true if read, false if unread) */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the notification (1 for high, 3 for low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
